1.description: Aggregate gross fund firm return, aggregate equity portfolio return, and aggregate unobserved return component. Panel A displays the evolution ofAggregate Gross Fund Firm ReturnandAggregate Equity PF Return. Panel B plotsAggregate Unobserved Return Componentover time. Our sample is the intersection of equity-oriented hedge fund firms from the Union Hedge Fund Database (constructed by combining the Eurekahedge, HFR, Morningstar, and Lipper TASS databases) and firms that report 13F long-equity holdings to the SEC. The sample period is January 1997 to December 2017.

2.description: Cumulative returns for trading based on unobserved performance. This figure displays cumulative returns from pursuing the UP-based trading strategy. We assume an initial investment of $1 at the beginning of 1997 and apply monthly rebalancing. Panel A shows how $1 invested in the different quintile UP portfolios grows over time on a risk-adjusted basis. Panel B plots the performance of the (5 – 1) UP spread, as well as the spreads based onGross Fund Firm PerformanceandEquity PF Performance, over time. Our sample is the intersection of equity-oriented hedge fund firms from the Union Hedge Fund Database (constructed by combining the Eurekahedge, HFR, Morningstar, and Lipper TASS databases) and firms that report 13F long-equity holdings to the SEC. The sample period is January 1997 to December 2017.

3.description: Gross Fund Firm PerformanceandUnobserved Performance: Long-term predictability. This figure plots the results of of Fama and MacBeth (1973) regressions of hedge fund firmi’s nine-factor alphas in yearst+ 1,t+ 2,t+ 3, andt+ 4 onGross Fund Firm Performance, Equity PF Performance, Unobserved Performance, and different fund firm characteristics in yeart. We plot the coefficient estimates onGross Fund Firm PerformanceandUnobserved Performancefor the different years for the full sample (Panel A), high-attention firms (Panel B), and low-attention firms (Panel C).
